title: Attacker round output (attack records + roll-up)
description: Top-level document a single attack round emits. PROVE-OR-FLAG split: records[] are proven, reproducible breakages; needs_judgment[] are unresolved/unprovable items. summary rolls up ASR@n, unique-finding count, and a severity histogram. Modeled on low-visibility-fix/analyzer-output.schema.json + research-brief §4. JSONL FORM: the validator CLI reads .jsonl as one record per line; the OPTIONAL roll-up line is identified by POSITIVE evidence — it carries at least one roll-up key (by_severity | budget_n | asr_at_n | unique_finding_count) OR an explicit {"type":"summary",...} marker, AND carries NONE of the record-only fields (status, proven, repro, oracle, regression_key, attack_class, observed, expected, invariant, independence_attestation). Any line carrying ANY record-only field is a RECORD — even one with no/empty `id` (incl. the duplicate-id-key case where JSON.parse keeps a trailing "") and even one carrying type:"summary" — so it reaches the gate and a malformed/no-id record is REJECTED there, never silently reclassified and dropped. The roll-up must be parsed EXACTLY ONCE: more than one roll-up line is REJECTED. A record's own `summary` field never reclassifies or drops it. When NO roll-up line is supplied, the CLI synthesizes the fallback summary FROM the records (consistent by construction); by_severity reconciliation only fires against a user-supplied summary, whose by_severity must use ONLY the {critical,major,minor} keys (the validator rejects unknown keys).

summary.description: Round roll-up the LOOP branches on. v0.2.0: the round emits a machine-readable round_verdict (the loop's STOP-CONDITION — clean⇒STOP, broke⇒fix-then-re-attack, inconclusive⇒loop-owner-decides) plus stop_reason and the DUAL hard budget (attempts: budget_n/attempts_used; token-consumption: max_tokens/tokens_used). carried_from_round records the prior attack round whose ledger THIS round inherited (the carry-forward discipline so a later round never cold-restarts and re-derives the whole attack plan). v0.3.0: the ATTACK-SCOPE CONTRACT — in_scope (>=1 free-form descriptors) declares which domains/layers were attacked; out_of_scope (may be empty) declares the excluded domains; every confirmed record's attack_scope MUST be an exact member of in_scope (the validator enforces this on a user-supplied summary). Optional context_digest is a short attestation of the context the round's attacks were grounded in. v0.3.1: attack_mode (debug|structural|both) sets the round's ALTITUDE — debug = concrete behavioral bug-hunt, structural = interrogate the project's LOGIC/ARCHITECTURE, both = structural-first then debug — and gates every record's attack_kind; context_sources (>=1 non-empty strings) attests where the round's context came from (user-provided / self-researched); scope_change (initial|stable|expanded|narrowed) records scope stability/incremental expansion; depth (int>=1) is the progressive-deepening level. These scope/context/mode checks (like round-verdict) fire only on a user-supplied summary, never the CLI's synthesized records-only fallback.

in_scope.description: v0.3.0 attack-scope contract: the declared domains/layers this round attacked, as >=1 RICH free-form descriptor strings (NOT a fixed enum), e.g. 'UI rendering errors', 'page navigation/logic transitions', 'logical soundness of the pricing argument'. Every confirmed record's attack_scope MUST be an exact member of this set (the validator enforces it on a user-supplied summary).

out_of_scope.description: v0.3.0: the declared EXCLUDED domains/layers (may be empty). Free-form descriptors, like in_scope. Observations the attacker found OUTSIDE the declared scope go in the TOP-LEVEL out_of_scope[] bucket (kept, not counted), not here — this is the scope declaration.

context_digest.description: v0.3.0 OPTIONAL lean machine handle: a short attestation of what context (claim/requirement, constraints, success criteria, what-counts-as-a-break, in/out-of-scope, prior rounds) the round's attacks were grounded in. When present the validator type-checks it (non-empty string); it is never required.

attack_mode.description: v0.3.1 the round's ALTITUDE (orthogonal to record.target.type). debug = a concrete behavioral bug-hunt (the v0.1–0.3.0 flow). structural = interrogate the project's LOGIC/ARCHITECTURE (design problems, coupling, logic-flow, missing/leaky abstractions, inconsistent patterns) — higher altitude. both = do STRUCTURAL FIRST, then debug (the structural-first ordering is a PROTOCOL discipline, not validator-enforced). Every confirmed record's attack_kind MUST be permitted by this mode (debug→only debug records; structural→only structural; both→either) — the validator enforces it.

context_sources.description: v0.3.1 MANDATORY: where THIS round's context came from, as >=1 non-empty strings (e.g. 'user-provided', 'self-researched: attack surface map', 'self-researched: module structure'). The attacker MUST NOT attack until scope is clear AND context is sufficient; if insufficient it SELF-RESEARCHES the project (the WHAT) and records that here.

scope_change.description: v0.3.1 scope stability: each round stays stable in scope OR expands INCREMENTALLY (never a wild jump). initial = round 1's first declared scope; stable = unchanged from last round; expanded = incrementally added surface; narrowed = focused down. The 'is the expansion really incremental' judgment is protocol/fresh-reader; the validator only checks the enum is valid.

depth.description: v0.3.1 progressive-deepening level within a scope (>=1). round>1 FIRST regression-checks prior records by regression_key, uses that resolution to FILL context, THEN goes DEEPER (depth increments) — never restart from scratch. Monotonic depth across rounds is a protocol discipline; the validator only checks depth >= 1.

round_verdict.description: The loop's STOP-CONDITION signal. broke ⟺ ≥1 confirmed/proven record (route to a fix round). clean = no proven break AND the plan ran to completion (stop_reason:plan_complete) — the loop's done/converged signal (NB: clean ≠ proven correct; it is 'no proven break within budget B'). inconclusive = no proven break BUT a budget cap was hit (stop_reason:budget_exhausted) — a qualified stop the loop owner decides on.

stop_reason.description: Why the round stopped. plan_complete = the attack plan ran to completion within both caps. budget_exhausted = a hard budget cap was reached (attempts_used>=budget_n OR tokens_used>=max_tokens) — exhaust-budget mode (one round reports ALL proven breaks for a batch fix, never stop-on-first).

max_tokens.description: Per-round token-consumption hard cap (NOT wall-clock). The round stops at whichever of budget_n (attempts) or max_tokens (tokens) is reached first.

tokens_used.description: Tokens consumed this round; must be <= max_tokens.

carried_from_round.description: The prior attack round whose ledger (surface map + attack tree + attempted-breaks + confirmed/fixed records) THIS round inherited. null ONLY at round 1; for round>1 it MUST be an integer with 1 <= carried_from_round < round (carry-forward discipline: a later round declares which prior round's ledger it built on, so it can't cold-restart and waste tokens re-deriving the plan).

out_of_scope.description: v0.3.0: observations the attacker found that are OUTSIDE the declared summary scope — KEPT (not lost) but NOT counted as findings and NOT subject to the confirmed-record gate (unlike records[]). Each item is an object (free-form; no proof/scope-tag requirements). This is how out-of-scope discoveries are preserved without polluting the finding count.

independence_attestation.description: PRODUCT mode: withheld MUST include 'implementation_source' + 'tdd_suite' (the validator enforces it) so the §1 firewall is auditable, and real_collaborator_at_seam is required on the record. IDEA mode: withheld is NOT required (the idea + its justification IS the input); instead derived_independently MUST be true (the critique was derived independently, not by adopting the proposer's defense as settled). derived_expected_from is required in BOTH modes (the source the counter/expected was derived from). The mode-conditional logic lives in the validator; the schema is permissive.

withheld.description: Context channels denied to the attacking subagent. In PRODUCT mode a confirmed record MUST include 'implementation_source' and 'tdd_suite' (the validator enforces this). In IDEA mode it is optional (the idea + its justification is the input).

derived_expected_from.description: The artifact 'expected' was derived from, independently. PRODUCT: the requirement (e.g. REQ-checkout-12), NOT the impl or its tests. IDEA: the claim/source the counter was derived from (e.g. the proposal's thesis).

derived_independently.description: v0.3.0 IDEA mode: STRUCTURAL attestation the critique was derived INDEPENDENTLY (did not adopt the proposer's defense/rebuttal as settled). The validator REQUIRES true on a confirmed idea-mode record.

steps.description: OR numbered steps from a KNOWN START STATE (exact element + value + timing).

minimized_input.description: The SHRUNK 1-minimal case — never the raw noisy discovery. Object/array/string/number all permitted; null/absent is rejected by the validator on confirmed records.

replayed_ok.description: STRUCTURAL attestation that the recorded repro was re-run and STILL re-triggered the breakage (checklist entry 13). The validator REQUIRES true on confirmed records; the fresh-reader checklist owns the true semantic re-check.

replays_passed.description: For flaky/non-deterministic targets: how many of replays_total the repro re-triggered (Q4 ASR@n threshold).

replays_total.description: Total replay attempts for a flaky target. proven requires replays_passed/replays_total >= the k/n threshold (default 1.0 for deterministic targets); below threshold routes to needs_judgment.

target.description: v0.3.0: type selects the MODE. 'product' = a running feature/function/endpoint (the v0.1–0.2 behavior). 'idea' = an argument/design/plan/thesis (debate con-side); statement holds the idea text and the product-only fields (version/model/checkpoint/system_prompt_hash) are optional/null.

type.description: The attack MODE. product → product oracles + the impl/tdd-withholding firewall + real_collaborator_at_seam. idea → idea oracles + claim/not_strawman/derived_independently; product-only fields not required.

name.description: feature/function/endpoint (product) or the idea's short name (idea) under attack. CJK / emoji / zero-width permitted.

statement.description: v0.3.0 IDEA mode: the idea/thesis text being attacked (optional).

attack_class.description: Free-form sub-classed enum (e.g. business_logic.out_of_sequence). Top family must be one of the §3 taxonomy families.

oracle.description: v0.3.0: mode-conditional. PRODUCT oracles (implicit|differential|metamorphic|control_vs_experiment|specified) attack a running target; IDEA oracles (counterexample|contradiction|unmet_assumption|scope_violation|infeasibility|missing_case) attack an argument/design. The union is listed here; the validator enforces the MODE-APPROPRIATE subset per target.type (a product oracle on an idea, or vice versa, is REJECTED).

attack_scope.description: v0.3.0 attack-scope contract: which declared in_scope domain THIS attack targeted. The validator REQUIRES a non-empty value on every confirmed record AND (on a user-supplied summary) that it is an EXACT member of summary.in_scope; the semantic 'is this really that domain' judgment stays with the fresh-reader.

attack_kind.description: v0.3.1 the KIND of this finding (orthogonal to target.type). debug = a concrete behavioral/logical bug (keeps the v0.3.0 product|idea debug gate: product → product oracle + real_collaborator_at_seam + withheld⊇{impl,tdd}; idea → claim + idea oracle + not_strawman). structural = a LOGIC/ARCHITECTURE design problem; it does NOT require impl-withholding or real_collaborator_at_seam (you must see the structure to critique it), but REQUIRES critique_basis + independence_attestation.derived_independently===true + observed!=expected + a STRUCTURAL oracle. The validator REQUIRES this on every confirmed record and REJECTS a kind not permitted by summary.attack_mode (debug→only debug; structural→only structural; both→either).

critique_basis.description: v0.3.1 STRUCTURAL records: the EXTERNAL design principle OR stated project goal the structure violates (e.g. 'single-responsibility', 'the stated goal that modules stay decoupled', 'consistent error-handling pattern'). REQUIRED + non-empty on a confirmed structural record (a vague 'this could be cleaner' with no concrete violated principle/goal is NOT a finding → needs_judgment). Not used by debug records.

claim.description: v0.3.0 IDEA mode: the steelmanned thesis the record attacks. REQUIRED on a confirmed idea-mode record (the validator enforces it). For an idea, observed != expected means expected = what the claim predicts, observed = the counter outcome.

not_strawman.description: v0.3.0 IDEA mode: STRUCTURAL attestation the critique attacks the ACTUAL steelmanned claim, not a misread. The validator REQUIRES true on a confirmed idea-mode record.

observed.description: What the target ACTUALLY did, verbatim. Any JSON type.

expected.description: Correct behavior derived INDEPENDENTLY from the requirement. Any JSON type.

non_tautology_check.description: Explicit proof the probe COULD have failed (real path, a correct impl would pass).

real_collaborator_at_seam.description: STRUCTURAL attestation that the attacked seam used a REAL collaborator, not a mock (checklist entry 14). The validator REQUIRES true on confirmed records; a fully-mocked path is zero-evidence.
